title: Rule-Async-JobScheduler
description: JSON schema for creating or updating a Pega Job Scheduler rule (Rule-Async-JobScheduler). Only authored and identity fields are included; system-managed fields are omitted.

pyRuleName.description: Internal rule name. Server-derived from pyPurpose — do not set directly.

pyPurpose.description: Rule identifier and class key; unique, case-insensitive. Ask the user for the rule name and use it here. The server uses this to populate pyRuleName.

pyBaseClass.description: Class the rule applies to. Use '@baseclass' for global (class-agnostic) scope.

pyRuleSet.description: Ruleset name the rule is saved into.

pyActivityName.description: Name of the activity to execute on each scheduled run.

pyNodeTypes.description: Map of node type keys the JS is eligible to run on. Never use node IDs. Use 'BackgroundProcessing' for standard background nodes.

pyRecurringFrequency.description: Schedule type. 'Startup' runs once on node start and suits per-node initialisation. 'Multiple times a day' minimum interval is 1 minute. 'Daily', 'Weekly', 'Monthly', 'Yearly' run on a calendar schedule.

pyApplicableTo.description: 'Cluster' — one node wins the distributed lock and runs the JS exactly once per cluster. Use for archival, aggregation, and dispatch jobs. 'Associated node' — every qualifying node runs the JS independently. Use only for per-node initialisation or monitoring; never for jobs that acquire cross-cluster DB locks.

pyAccessGroupContext.description: 'true' — activity resolves against the SRC application stack (default). 'false' — activity runs under the named pyAccessGroup. Use 'false' when CDH or rule-specific context is needed and a named access group should be used instead of SRC.

pyAccessGroup.description: Access group for activity execution. Always required — server returns 'Value cannot be blank' if omitted. Set pyAccessGroupContext to 'false' and provide a named access group (e.g. 'MyOrg-MyApp:Administrators').

pyRecurrencePattern.description: Schedule timing configuration. Required when pyRecurringFrequency is not 'Startup'. CAUTION: never include pyRecurrencePattern in an update-rule payload unless you explicitly intend to change the schedule — deep merge will overwrite the existing pyStartTime.

pyStartTime.description: Time at which the schedule starts, expressed in the timezone named by pyUseTimeZone — NOT in UTC. Format: HHmmss (6 digits, no separators, no timezone suffix), e.g. '170000' for 17:00:00 local time. The server stores the value normalised to UTC internally, but you must supply the wall-clock time in the target timezone. Do NOT pre-convert to UTC before sending — that will cause the job to fire at the wrong local time. Server rejects 'HH:MM:SS GMT' strings as invalid.

pyUseTimeZone.description: IANA timezone ID for the schedule. Use 'GMT' for UTC. Examples: 'GMT', 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London'. Defaults to 'GMT' when pySystemDefaultTimeZone is false. Do NOT use display names or offset strings.

pyDailyInterval.description: Number of days between runs. Required when pyRecurringFrequency is 'Daily', e.g. '1' for every day.

pyIsEnabled.description: Set 'false' to disable the JS without deleting it.
